title: drawLine demo
content_html:
drawLine demo

Below is an implementation for a new function, demo_drawLine(), which draws a line for each style (“quick”, “natural”, “wild”) in a manner similar to demo_createLineBounded(). In this example, we use the existing drawLine() function and simply loop through the styles while spacing the start points vertically:

void demo_drawLine() { // Define the styles you want to demonstrate. String[] styles = {"quick", "natural", "wild"};

// Set a fixed starting x-coordinate. float startX = 200;

// Calculate vertical spacing based on the number of styles. int numStyles = styles.length; float ySpacing = height / (numStyles + 1);

// Set text properties for labeling. textAlign(RIGHT, CENTER); textSize(12); fill(0);

// Loop through each style, draw the line, and label it. for (int i = 0; i < numStyles; i++) { // Calculate the start point for this style. PVector start = new PVector(startX, ySpacing * (i + 1));

*// Draw the line using the specified style.*
drawLine(start, styles[i]);

*// Optionally, add a label (the drawLine function already labels the line).*
*// If you want an additional label, you could do:*
*// text("Style: " + styles[i], start.x - 20, start.y);*

} }

How It Works • Styles Array: The function defines an array of style strings—“quick”, “natural”, and “wild”. • Spacing: It uses a fixed starting x-coordinate and evenly spaces the y-coordinates across the height of the canvas. • Loop: For each style, it creates a start point, calls drawLine() (which uses setLineBoundary() internally with the given style), and labels the line.

Simply call demo_drawLine() (for example, in your setup or key press handler) to see a demonstration of the three different styles in one drawing.
